skill.name: startup-financial-modeling
skill.description: Build 3-5 year startup financial models with revenue forecasts, cost structures, cash flow, runway, metrics, and funding scenarios.
skill.summary: Create structured startup financial projections for planning, fundraising, and operational decisions.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown backticks, finance prose, and formulas as external commands, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance. Manual review found no executable code, network activity, secret access, or prompt-injection attempts in SKILL.md, so the findings are dismissed as false positives.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Treated as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The flagged lines are Markdown code fences, inline references, or financial formulas. They do not invoke a shell, Ruby backticks, subprocesses, or external commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding content is plain Markdown documentation and financial-model examples. No executable script, shell command, or user-controlled command construction is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Finance Text Matched Weak Crypto Patterns
low_findings.description: The flagged lines contain ordinary financial-modeling prose, including description text, decision support, design work, and contraction MRR. No cryptographic algorithm or library usage is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact lines are domain text rather than code. There is no evidence of MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or any cryptographic operation.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Cash Flow Lines Treated as Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The flagged lines describe operating expense timing in a cash-flow model. They do not collect host information, enumerate files, inspect processes, or query system configuration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matches are inside financial planning examples. No system command or reconnaissance intent is visible in the referenced context.

content.value_statement: Startup teams need clear projections to plan hiring, fundraising, revenue growth, and cash runway. This skill structures a practical financial model with assumptions, scenarios, metrics, and investor-ready planning outputs.
actual_capabilities: Defines revenue models using customer cohorts, ARPU, retention, expansion, and churn assumptions.
Builds operating expense structures across COGS, sales and marketing, R&D, and G&A.
Calculates cash flow, monthly burn, runway, and funding needs from stated assumptions.
Creates conservative, base, and optimistic scenarios for 3-5 year planning.
Explains startup metrics such as CAC, LTV, gross margin, burn multiple, and revenue per employee.
Models fundraising amounts, dilution, use of funds, and milestone-based runway planning.
limitations: It provides modeling guidance and formulas, not certified financial, tax, legal, or investment advice.
It needs user-supplied assumptions, historical data, or benchmarks to produce useful projections.
It does not include executable spreadsheet files in the scanned file tree.
It does not fetch live market data, company comparables, or current investor benchmarks.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Fundraising Model
use_cases.description: Create a 36-month model that links revenue, costs, headcount, runway, and funding needs.

use_cases.title: Validate Operating Plans
use_cases.description: Stress test hiring, marketing spend, gross margin, and burn assumptions before committing budget.

use_cases.title: Compare Business Model Economics
use_cases.description: Outline SaaS, marketplace, or e-commerce drivers and identify the metrics that shape each model.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Model
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 3-year startup financial model for a [business type]. Use these assumptions: starting cash, pricing, customer growth, churn, gross margin, hiring plan, and monthly expenses.

prompt_templates.title: Estimate Runway
prompt_templates.prompt: Estimate monthly burn and runway from this plan: current cash, monthly revenue, payroll, COGS, sales and marketing spend, R&D spend, G&A spend, and expected hiring dates.

prompt_templates.title: Build Scenario Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Build conservative, base, and optimistic financial scenarios for the next 36 months. Show the assumptions that change revenue, expenses, cash runway, and funding need.

prompt_templates.title: Model Fundraising and Dilution
prompt_templates.prompt: Model a fundraising plan for this startup. Include pre-money valuation, investment amount, post-money valuation, dilution, use of funds, runway extension, and milestone coverage.

output_examples.input: Create a 3-year SaaS projection for $50 ARPU, 20 new customers per month, 4 percent monthly churn, and $500,000 starting cash.
output_examples.output: A structured SaaS forecast with monthly customer cohorts, MRR, ARR, churn impact, gross margin, expenses, cash balance, runway, and key assumptions.

output_examples.input: Compare conservative, base, and optimistic growth plans for a marketplace startup.
output_examples.output: A scenario summary showing GMV, take rate, net revenue, buyer and seller growth, contribution margin, burn, and funding need for each case.

output_examples.input: Plan a $5 million seed round and show the dilution and use of funds.
output_examples.output: A financing summary with pre-money valuation, post-money valuation, dilution percentage, runway extension, and spending allocation by function.

best_practices: Separate assumptions from calculations so every forecast driver is easy to review.
Use monthly projections for the first 24-36 months because runway and hiring depend on timing.
Create at least three scenarios and document which assumptions change in each scenario.
anti_patterns: Do not hide aggressive growth assumptions inside formulas without explaining them.
Do not model revenue without cash collection timing, churn, gross margin, and operating expenses.
Do not treat the model as financial advice without review from qualified advisors.

faq.question: What business models does this skill support?
faq.answer: It covers SaaS, marketplace, e-commerce, and general startup models using revenue drivers, costs, cash flow, and scenario planning.

faq.question: Can it create investor-ready projections?
faq.answer: It can structure investor-facing projections, assumptions, metrics, and funding scenarios. Users should validate outputs before sharing them.

faq.question: Does it generate spreadsheet files?
faq.answer: The scanned skill provides guidance and formulas. It does not include an executable spreadsheet template in the available file tree.

faq.question: What inputs should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide pricing, customer growth, churn, gross margin, starting cash, expenses, hiring plans, and fundraising assumptions.

faq.question: Can it calculate runway and burn rate?
faq.answer: Yes. It describes monthly cash flow, burn rate, ending cash, runway, and funding need calculations.

faq.question: Is this a replacement for professional finance advice?
faq.answer: No. It supports planning and analysis, but professional review is needed for tax, legal, accounting, and investment decisions.
